text: Community-acquired pneumonia: empiric therapy with amoxicillin 1g TID or doxycycline for outpatients without comorbidities. CURB-65 score guides admission decisions.
For moderate CAP, consider amoxicillin plus macrolide or respiratory fluoroquinolone. Reassess at 48-72 hours.
Hypertension: target BP <130/80 for most adults. First-line agents include thiazide diuretics, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or CCBs.
Type 2 diabetes: metformin first-line unless contraindicated. HbA1c target generally <7% for most adults.
Atrial fibrillation: CHA2DS2-VASc score guides anticoagulation. DOACs preferred over warfarin unless mechanical valve.
COPD exacerbation: SABA plus SAMA, systemic corticosteroids, antibiotics if purulent sputum.
Uncomplicated UTI in women: nitrofurantoin 100mg BID x 5 days or TMP-SMX as alternative.
AI-CDSS may be classified as Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) if providing diagnostic or treatment recommendations requiring Health Canada approval.
